questions.text: When it comes to your gaming setup and session habits — do you prefer playing on console, PC, or both? And when you actually sit down to game, are you someone who plays for longer stretches in one go, or do you prefer shorter sessions (30-60 minutes) where you can jump in and out?

When you think about life planning — beyond financial goals — do you have a vision for where you want to be in 3 years, 5 years, 10 years? Is this something you actively think about and document, or do you prefer keeping it loose and seeing how things unfold?

When you read fiction versus non-fiction — do you seek out different things for each, or is it all just 'reading' to you? And are there any specific authors, genres, or types of books you keep coming back to for comfort re-reads?

What's your relationship with digital privacy and data security? Do you use VPNs, ad blockers, specific browsers, or have any routines around protecting your online footprint — or are you more in the 'they already have everything, so why stress' camp?

When you're consuming content for fun — not podcasts or music, but doom-scrolling or browsing — what's your go-to? Do you spend time on Reddit, YouTube, TikTok, Twitter/X, news apps, or do you find yourself mostly doing other things with that free time?
